message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Failing test: "quote for 2 runes should have 50 G base premium (2 x 25 G, no block)"
Expected behavior: processScenario with 2 runes returns { results: [{ premium: 60 }] } (50 base + 5 first insurance + 5 fee)
Current error: expected { results: [{ premium: NaN }] } to deeply equal { results: [{ premium: 60 }] }
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts

Current implementation only handles items[0] and has no "rune" in BASE_PREMIUMS.

Two problems to fix:
1. Add "rune" to BASE_PREMIUMS with value 25
2. Sum base premiums across ALL items, not just items[0]

The first insurance surcharge (10%) applies to the total base premium sum, then add the 5 G fee.

Keep it minimal. The previous tests with single items must still pass.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor after completion.

content.text: I'll start by reading the current test file and implementation file to understand the exact state of things.

content.text: Now I can see both problems clearly. Let me run the tests first to confirm the current failure.

input.description: Run tests to confirm current failure

content.text:
Good. The failure is confirmed: `NaN` because `BASE_PREMIUMS["rune"]` is `undefined`, and the code only looks at `items[0]`. Two minimal changes are needed:

1. Add `"rune": 25` to `BASE_PREMIUMS`
2. Sum base premiums across all items instead of just `items[0]`

Let me apply these changes.

content.text: All 6 tests pass, 34 remain as todos. Let me confirm the final state of the implementation file.

Green Phase Complete:

**Implementation**: Two changes made to `/home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-21_15-29-15_claim-office-example-mapping_v4.1-testlist-scope-fix_opus-4-6-portkey-no-thinking-5/src/claim-office.ts`:
1. Added `rune: 25` to the `BASE_PREMIUMS` lookup table (line 9)
2. Changed `calculatePremium` to sum base premiums across all items using a `for` loop instead of only reading `items[0]` (lines 17-19)

**Result**: All tests now pass (6 passing, 34 todo)

**Approach**: This is minimal because both changes are strictly required by the failing test. The `rune` entry is needed so `BASE_PREMIUMS["rune"]` resolves to 25 instead of `undefined`. The loop over all items is needed because the test provides 2 runes and expects their premiums summed (2 x 25 = 50 base). The loop also naturally preserves the behavior for the 4 existing single-item tests (a loop over 1 item is equivalent to accessing `items[0]`). No extra logic, no optimization, no future features added.

Proceeding to Refactor phase.
